2. **"Mountaintop" Speech (1968)**: In this speech, delivered the night before his assassination, King spoke about the importance of perseverance in the face of opposition. 3. **"Letter from Birmingham Jail" (1963)**: In this letter, King wrote about the importance of perseverance in the face of injustice. He said, "For years now, I have heard the word 'Wait!' It rings in the ear of every Negro with piercing familiarity. This 'Wait' has almost always meant 'Never.' We must come to see, with one of our distinguished jurists, that 'justice too long delayed is justice denied.'"

4. **"Where Do We Go From Here?" Speech (1967)**: In this speech, King emphasized the importance of perseverance in the face of adversity. He said, "We are coming to see, with one of our distinguished jurists, that 'justice too long delayed is justice denied.' We are coming to see that the time for waiting is over, that the time for action is now."

5. **"Give Us the Ballot" Speech (1957)**: In this speech, King emphasized the importance of perseverance in the face of opposition. He said, "We are not here to beg the white man for the right to vote. We are here to demand that he give it to us."

But remember with this world, that none of us is as important as all of us together."

* **Malcolm X**: While his views on nonviolent resistance were different from King's, Malcolm X emphasized the importance of perseverance in the face of adversity and oppression.

These leaders, along with Martin Luther King Jr., demonstrate the power of perseverance in the face of adversity and the importance of holding onto hope and working towards a better future.

2. **"Mountaintop" Speech (1968)**: In this speech, delivered the night before his assassination, King spoke about the importance of perseverance in the face of opposition. He said, "Like anybody, I would like to live a long life. Longevity has its place. But I'm not concerned about that now. I just want to do God's will. And He's allowed me to go up to the mountain. 3. **"Letter from Birmingham Jail" (1963)**: In this letter, King wrote about the importance of perseverance in the face of injustice. He said, "For years now, I have heard the word 'Wait!' It rings in the ear of every Negro with piercing familiarity. This 'Wait' has almost always meant 'Never.' We must come to see, with one of our distinguished jurists, that 'justice too long delayed is justice denied.'"

4. **"Where Do We Go From Here?" Speech (1967)**: In this speech, King emphasized the importance of perseverance in the face of adversity. He said, "We are coming to see, with one of our distinguished jurists, that 'justice too long delayed is justice denied.' We are coming to see that the time for waiting is over, that the time for action is now."

5. **"Give Us the Ballot" Speech (1957)**: In this speech, King emphasized the importance of perseverance in the face of opposition. He said, "We are not here to beg the white man for the right to vote. We are here to demand that he give it to us."

If you want to be recognized—wonderful. If you want to be first—string quartet, first violin, first trumpet—that is wonderful.
